itemName.label: Item Name
itemName.placeholder: Airbnb (AirBnC) — Book accommodations anywhere in 5 seconds

description.label: Item Description
description.placeholder: Airbnb is a accommodation sharing platform that allows travelers to book hosts' spare rooms through an app, and hosts to monetize their extra space. Recorded 250 bookings and 38% revisit rate in 6 weeks of beta. Automated the entire 'booking-check-in-payment' process with trust score-based matching algorithm and real-time secure payments. Goal to achieve 150 cities, 10,000 hosts, and 100,000 bookings within 18 months.

itemName.label: Item Name
itemName.placeholder: Airbnb (AirBnC) — Book accommodations anywhere in 5 seconds

difference.label: Differentiation
difference.placeholder: Example) Lyft: Largest competitor in US and Canada. Didi: China's largest mobility service, weak international market presence with security concerns. Limited interface and language. Ola: India service. Inconsistent service quality and vehicle condition. Grab: Southeast Asia ridesharing company with extensive ecosystem in food delivery market as well. Differentiation

businessPlan.label: Business Plan
businessPlan.placeholder: Example) 1. Select service areas for operation then gradually expand. 2. Reflect Beta test feedback to improve app UI/UX. 3. Strengthen verification procedures and safety features for driver and user safety. 4. Plan to expand services through partnerships with local businesses.

marketingStrategy.label: Marketing Strategy
marketingStrategy.placeholder: Example) Plan to market through various social media platforms with content like user reviews, service tutorial videos, and promotional events. Also plan to share service introduction videos and customer success stories on YouTube, and conduct region-specific promotions and Q&A sessions in local communities and forums (Naver Cafe, Daum Cafe). Branded

developmentStatus.label: Development Status
developmentStatus.placeholder: Example) 1. Secured 100 pre-booking customers through MVP launch. 2. Completed advanced matching algorithm, reducing wait time and improving efficiency through optimal matching algorithm between users and drivers. 3. Improving service based on user feedback through Beta test.

step4.title: Draft for Investment and Review

item.header: Create your Core Plan in 3 steps
item.optionalHeader:
Optional inputs —
Your Core Plan is ready to generate anytime

description.label: Product Description
description.placeholder: AirBnC is an accommodation-sharing platform that allows travelers to book rooms from local hosts, enabling hosts to monetize their extra space. In just 6 weeks of beta, it achieved 2,500 bookings with a 38% matching rate, and has automated the entire 'Booking-Check-in-Payment' process with a trust-based matching algorithm and real-time secure payments. The goal is to reach 100,000 bookings from 10,000 hosts across 150 cities within 18 months.

color.label: Theme Color
color.description: Please select a color that best represents your product. This color will be used as the base for generating charts and images.

validation.nameAndDescriptionRequired: Please write item name and description.

header.line1: Fill out this page to complete your

painPoint.label: Pain Point
painPoint.placeholder: During conferences or festivals, hotel prices in the city can surge up to 3-4 times their usual rate, placing a heavy burden on travelers. Even at these high prices, guests are often left with generic accommodations that lack any local experience. Currently, there is no viable alternative in the market to solve this problem.
painPoint.tooltip:
What specific and urgent “Pain Point” are customers willing to spend their time and money to solve? Please describe it in the format: [Who] + [In what situation] + [What difficulties they face].
To make your case more persuasive, also explain how customers are currently addressing the problem and what limitations existing solutions have.

background.label: Our Story
background.placeholder: The idea of "sharing spare rooms" came from experiencing the surge in accommodation prices during a conference. We secured our first booking with an MVP that consisted of just an air mattress in a living room.
background.tooltip: Explain honestly what led you to start this business — whether it was a personal experience or a market opportunity you spotted. Investors care deeply about the story behind “Why is this team the right one to solve this problem?” Make sure to clearly convey your motivation and what makes your team uniquely qualified.

features.label: Key Features
features.placeholder:
1. Property Search: Users input their desired dates and criteria to see a list of recommended local accommodations.
2. Booking Request & Confirmation: When a user requests to book a property, the reservation is confirmed either upon the host's acceptance or automatically.
3. Location Sharing: Guests and hosts can share their locations in real-time and communicate via an integrated map feature.
4. Payment & Review System: Payment is processed automatically upon booking confirmation, and a trust score is calculated based on mutual reviews after the stay.
features.tooltip: List up to five key features of your service or product. Describe each one from the customer’s point of view, focusing on the value or benefit they receive from it.

mission.label: Mission & Vision
mission.placeholder:
- Mission: To connect travelers with spare spaces around the world through technology, offering unique and affordable accommodation experiences.
- Vision: To create a world where anyone can not just 'stay' anywhere, but 'belong' anywhere.
mission.tooltip:
- Mission: A mission is a present-day commitment that explains what we do and how we do it as a company. (Example: We solve XX problems using OOO technology.)
- Vision: A vision is the ultimate future state that will be realized when our mission is fulfilled. (Example: A world where everyone can live without worrying about OO.)

header.line1: Fill out this page to outline your

target.label: Target Market & Customer
target.placeholder:
Target Market: Within the global travel community (a $150B market), we are initially targeting Gen Z travelers who seek authentic, exploratory experiences beyond non-competitive niche markets. The estimated size of this initial market is approximately ₩500 billion KRW.
Target Customer: Travelers aged 29-38 who use social media to find local hotspots and are dissatisfied with the high commission fees and commercialized content on existing platforms.
target.tooltip: Describe the size of the overall market and the initial target market you plan to focus on, including specific figures. Then, define the profile of customers who would need your product the most in this market, using: [Age group] + [Behavior patterns] + [Needs or pain points].

rival.label: Competitors
rival.placeholder:
Booging: A weekend-focused booking platform, primarily partnered with hotels and resorts rather than individual hosts, which makes direct communication between guests and hosts difficult.
Acoba: A short-term rental platform centered in Southeast Asia, which suffers from low-quality reviews and inconsistent property management standards.
rival.tooltip: Select 2–4 key competitors and briefly analyze each competitor's core strengths and weaknesses.

profit.label: Unique Selling Points
profit.placeholder:
Unique Community Experience: We go beyond simple accommodation by offering authentic local experiences hosted by locals themselves, such as "local tours" and "cooking classes."
Trust-Based Matching: Our proprietary "Trust Score" algorithm, which incorporates past reviews and response rates, optimally matches guests with the most suitable hosts.
Transparent Pricing: We build trust by displaying the final price from the initial search stage, not just at the final booking step, eliminating any hidden fees.
profit.tooltip: What unique strengths set our service apart — ones that competitors cannot easily replicate? From perspectives such as [technological advantage], [unique business model], and [differentiated experience], list 3–4 specific key differentiators.

revenueModel.label: Revenue Model
revenueModel.placeholder:
- Transaction Fees: We take a 10% commission fee on the booking value paid by the guest.
- Subscription Fees: We offer premium features for hosts, such as priority listing, for a monthly subscription fee of ₩9,900.
- Commission Fees: We charge a 15% commission fee for brokering local experiences and content operated by hosts, such as "local tours."
revenueModel.tooltip:
Explain how your business makes money by outlining all core revenue streams. For each revenue model, clearly describe who pays, for what, when, and how much
— for example: transaction fees, monthly/annual subscriptions, advertising revenue, etc.

strategy.label: Marketing & Sales Strategy
strategy.placeholder:
Acquisition through Content: We will collaborate with travel YouTubers who appeal to our target audience (Gen Z) with "authentic localism" to secure initial brand awareness through genuine travel content.
Activation through First Booking: We will provide new users who sign up via our content with a limited-edition discount coupon, dramatically lowering the barrier to making their first booking.
Viral Growth through Referrals: We will expand our customer base at a low cost through a referral program where both the referrer and the new user receive credits upon the friend's first booking.
strategy.tooltip:
How will you design the full customer journey — from [① discovering your product → ② trying it for the first time → ③ sharing it with others]?
Discovery: Through which channels will customers find out about your product? (e.g., Instagram, YouTube, online communities, paid ads)
First use: What will encourage them to try it? (e.g., referral codes, discount coupons, free trials)
Spread: Who will help spread the word? (e.g., influencers, community partners, direct sales by your team)

header.line1: Fill out this page to define your

currentStatus.label: Current Progress
currentStatus.placeholder:
- Incorporation & Team Formation: Incorporated in Jan 2024 with 3 co-founders (2 developers, 1 editor).
- Product Development (MVP/Alpha): After an 8-month development period, our service has been successfully stabilized as of Jan 2025, with continuous improvements based on market feedback.
- Key Metrics: Achieved 250 bookings with a 38% matching rate within 6 weeks of beta. Current traction includes over 1,000 cumulative bookings and 500 registered hosts.
currentStatus.tooltip: Showcase what you’ve accomplished so far and the actions you took to get here — this is where you prove your execution capability.  Summarize your key achievements in areas such as [product development], [team building], [key metrics achieved], and [government projects or funding secured].

plan.label: 3-Year Milestones
plan.placeholder:
~ Q4 2025: Establish Domestic Market Presence.
- Official product launch and system enhancement.
- Secure 20,000 cumulative guests and 50,000 registered users by year-end.
~ H1 2026: Expand into Southeast Asian Market.
- Introduce AI-based personalized recommendations and a Host Protection Program.
- Growth: Enter Thai and Vietnamese markets, achieving 5,000 monthly bookings.
~ 2027: Prepare for Global Expansion.
- Product (Validation): Build the foundation and partnerships for a future M&A or IPO.
plan.tooltip: Outline your key milestones for the next 1–3 years, organized by timeline.  Include specific goals across major areas such as product development, growth, and market expansion.

sales.label: Revenue Plan
sales.placeholder:
- Year 1: ₩250M KRW / Year 2: ₩1.5B KRW / Year 3: ₩9.0B KRW
- (Basis: Year 3 projection is based on 4,200 paying customers × ₩100,000 ARPU per month).
sales.tooltip: Present your annual revenue targets for the next 3 years. To increase the credibility of your plan, include key assumptions such as target number of paying customers, average revenue per user (ARPU), and other core metrics used to calculate these targets.

cost.label: Use of Funds
cost.placeholder:
Personnel (50%): Salaries for core team members essential for development and operations (developers, designers, marketers).
Marketing (30%): Costs for influencer collaborations, content creation, and referral programs to acquire early users and hosts.
Operating Expenses (20%): Essential service operation costs, including server fees, payment gateway (PG) commissions, and other SaaS tool subscriptions.
cost.tooltip:
Show a main cost structure. List the top 3–4 cost drivers in order of importance, and provide a brief description for each
— for example: personnel costs, marketing expenses, server costs, transaction fees, etc.

investment.label: Funding Plan
investment.placeholder: Our goals are comparable to similar pre-Series A startups. The funds will be used to hire 2 additional developers (₩300M) and for initial marketing (₩200M). Estimated remainder: ₩500M.
investment.tooltip:
If you plan to raise investment, specify the following:
- Target funding amount
- Detailed allocation plan for how the funds will be used
- Runway (the period your business can operate with this funding)

header.line1: Fill out this page to outline your

goal.label: R&D Goals
goal.placeholder: To develop a system that proactively identifies and warns against potential safety threats (e.g., inappropriate messages, high-risk accommodations) to ensure a best-in-class user journey. This system will block fraudulent transactions and account takeover attempts in real-time with over 99% accuracy. Our goal is to reduce safety-related customer service inquiries by 50% and maximize platform trust.
goal.tooltip: Clearly describe what problem your product solves and what value it creates. Include measurable, quantitative goals relevant to your industry — for example: 20% performance improvement, 15% increase in production yield, or 99% algorithm accuracy.

tech.label: Key Technologies
tech.placeholder:
- Natural Language Processing (NLP): Utilizes a deep learning model (BERT-based) to analyze user messages, detecting hidden aggression, emotional distress, and other risk signals.
- Anomaly Detection: Employs machine learning to identify unusual booking and login patterns that deviate from normal user behavior, thereby preventing account fraud.
- Graph Neural Networks (GNN): Models the relationships between users, properties, and bookings as a graph to predict and detect organized fraudulent activities.
tech.tooltip: Describe the scientific principles, engineering design, core algorithms, or proprietary formulations/manufacturing methods that underpin your technology.

infra.label: R&D Resources
infra.placeholder:
- Data: An internal dataset of over 1 million anonymized user messages, booking logs, and transaction data, including 5,000 labeled instances of fraud/safety incidents.
- Infrastructure (H/W): Cloud-based GPU servers (NVIDIA A10G class) for model training and deployment.
- Software (S/W): Python, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, Apache Spark.
infra.tooltip: What are the key resources required to implement your technology? List the essential elements for R&D in detail, such as [main components/materials], [core data], [specialized equipment/facilities], [development software], and [specialized research personnel], according to your field.

status.label: R&D Progress
status.placeholder: We have completed the initial data acquisition and prior art/literature review stages. An automated pipeline for internal data collection, anonymization, and preprocessing has been established. We plan to finalize risk signal definitions and data labeling by Q4 2025, at which point we will commence prototype model development.
status.tooltip:
What stage is your R&D at? Provide specific details for each stage
- Completed work (past achievements)
- Ongoing work (current activities)
- Planned work (near-future actions)

copyright.label: Intellectual Property
copyright.placeholder:
An AI-Based User Safety Prediction System and Method Thereof
- Type: Patent
- Application No.: 10-2025-0012345
- Status: Pending
copyright.tooltip: List any intellectual property (IP) you have secured or are in the process of filing. For each item, please include its name, type (e.g., Patent, Trademark), application/registration number, and current status (e.g., Granted, Pending).

confirmModal.title: Generate Core Plan?
description: The AI will generate a Core Plan based on your input.
The original document created can be modified in a later chat.
